summary: SK Hynix Inc.’s American depositary receipts are indicated to open 21% above their offering price, after the South Korean memory chipmaker raised $26.5 billion in the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company.

We also discussed why Meta can cut $10 billion from a $22 billion IRS tax case in connection with IP offshoring. title: Americans Are Stuck in a Rut — And a Lack of Fun Might Be to Blame
summary:
America appears to be suffering from a fun shortage. From the rising cost of concert tickets, to bowling alley and nightclub closures, to venue shortages for kids to play in local sports leagues, the fun deficit is real and it's making Americans more isolated and lonelier than previous generations.

Bloomberg's Ben Steverman joins Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to explore some easy solutions to bring fun back into our everyday lives. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Heads for Weekly Loss as Traders Consider Iran, Rates Path
summary: Gold headed for a weekly loss as investors assessed renewed fighting in the Middle East and the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

title: Stocks Waver Before Earnings as SK Hynix to Debut: Markets Wrap
summary: A rally that drove stocks to a striking distance of their record highs wavered in the countdown to the earnings season, with traders also getting ready for SK Hynix Inc.’s debut after a blockbuster share sale.

title: Cencosud Shares Dive as Strategic Plan Yet to Boost Earnings
summary: More than a year into a new strategic plan at Chile’s Cencosud SA, the retailer’s shares have slumped and the company is asking investors for patience.

title: Morgan Stanley Sees Pressure Rising on Chipmakers’ Pricing Power
summary: Morgan Stanley’s Lisa Shalett urged investors to be cautious on chipmakers as evidence mounts that their pricing power is limited, suggesting that the stocks may have rallied too far on optimism around artificial-intelligence spending.

title: La dette cachée freine le développement du Sénégal: Next Africa
summary: Les grands projets d’infrastructure devaient incarner la transformation du Sénégal en l’une des économies les plus dynamiques d’Afrique de l’Ouest.

title: US Stock Futures Slip After Tech Rally, SK Hynix Starts Trading
summary: US equities were moving between losses and gains Friday as investors digested the record US listing of South Korea’s SK Hynix Inc. and prepared for the start of second-quarter earnings season.

title: A Month After SpaceX IPO, Tesla’s Stock Keeps Holding Its Ground
summary: In the weeks leading up to SpaceX’s monster IPO, many Wall Street pros feared the deal represented an existential threat for Elon Musk’s other giant, Tesla Inc. But after a month in the stock market, the outcome is far from clear.

title: Prediction Market Bets Surge on World Cup Mania
summary: Rabid interest in the World Cup has helped to drive more than $3 billion of wagers on a prediction market backed by Robinhood Markets Inc. and Susquehanna International Group, which launched just a few months ago.

title: SK Hynix Set to Make Record-Setting US Debut
summary: SK Hynix Inc. raised $26.5 billion in its American depositary share offering, the largest ever US first-time share sale by a foreign company. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Retail Traders Chase Shiny Objects But Refuse to Bet on S&P 500
summary: Retail traders, some of the staunchest supporters of the stock market this decade, are showing signs of waning conviction.

title: Oil Steadies as US, Iran Continue Talks After Renewed Fighting
summary: Oil steadied as the US and Iran continued talks despite a flare-up in fighting that strained their ceasefire and deterred tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: AlphaQuest Founder Lost Control at Quant Fund, Investor Alleges
summary: AlphaQuest founder Nigol Koulajian admitted losing control of trading at his $2 billion quantitative hedge fund before announcing plans to shut it down, an institutional investor claimed in a lawsuit.

title: Macro Hedge Fund Deem Raised $1 Billion From Abu Dhabi’s ADIC
summary: Asfandyar Nadeem’s hedge fund has received about $1 billion in new capital from an Abu Dhabi investor, amid a surge in appetite for macro strategies and as the Gulf city accelerates its drive to become a key backer of the industry.

title: Foreign Ownership of UK Firms Soared 35% Amid Glut of Deals
summary: The number of British companies controlled by overseas firms soared 35% in the years after the outbreak of Covid, as cheap valuations drove a dealmaking boom.

title: Czech Price Pressures Warrant Increased Caution for Central Bank
summary: The Czech central bank said monetary policy caution remains warranted, with the headline inflation rate likely to return to or “slightly above” the 2% target this year following a recent drop.

title: Delta Reaffirms Profit Guidance as Premium Demand Rises
summary: Delta Air Lines Inc. reaffirmed its full-year profit guidance and said strong demand for premium, corporate and international travel helped offset the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history, Stephen Trent, president and founder of SDT Capital Advisors, says the earnings and guidance were solid and shows the carrier surprised some on Wall Street. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Does Not Want All Out War in Iran, Cook Says
summary: Steven Cook, Council on Foreign Relations Middle East Senior Fellow, says it's unclear how President Donald Trump will "finish the job" in Iran. But Cook says Trump does not want to see a return to all out combat. Cook speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Cómo criar hijos en una de las capitales más calurosas de Europa
summary: Desde elegir el momento para ir al parque hasta tomar duchas frías, criar hijos en Madrid ofrece un adelanto de cómo el cambio climático está transformando la vida familiar en toda Europa. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Shein Gets Approval From Chinese Regulator for Hong Kong IPO
summary: Shein Global Holdings Ltd. got approval from the Chinese regulator to move ahead with its planned initial public offering in Hong Kong, capping a yearslong effort by the fast-fashion giant to go public.

title: Oil Product Markets Are the Real Hormuz Pain Point
summary: While crude has nearly erased its wartime rally, refined fuels are increasingly tight.

title: Echostar Bulls Say It Offers Traders Discounted SpaceX Exposure
summary: SpaceX may finally be within reach for public-market investors, but analysts say there’s a cheaper opportunity to get exposure to shares of the space technology company through EchoStar Corp.

title: SK Hynix's Blockbuster Debut; US Says Iran Talks Continue | Bloomberg Brief 07/10/2026
summary: SK Hynix raises $26.5 billion in its ADR offering, the largest first-time US share sale by a foreign company. A US official says talks with Iran on a peace deal continue. Seema Shah of Principal Asset Management discusses Federal Reserve Chair Warsh naming leaders of the five task forces. Nadia Martin Wiggen of Svelland Capital looks at the volatile week in oil. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Corn Drops as Heat Threat Eases Ahead of Key US Supply Report
summary: Corn futures fell for a third day as the immediate threat of extreme heat in the US eased. Traders are also awaiting the US Department of Agriculture’s WASDE report, due later Friday.

title: Inflation Is a Tailwind for European Stocks, Morgan Stanley Says
summary: European equities stand to benefit from higher inflation, according to Morgan Stanley’s Marina Zavolock, who says the market underestimates the region’s exposure to sectors that profit from rising prices. "We have a lot of real asset exposure, we have banks exposure,” Zavolock, the firm’s chief European equity strategist, tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SMFG Said to Weigh Strategy for India Assets Including Yes Bank
summary: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc. is evaluating its India strategy as it looks for ways to consolidate its presence in one of the world’s fastest-growing banking markets, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: BioOrbit CEO on Developing Cancer Treatments in Zero Gravity
summary:
Katie King, cofounder and CEO of BioOrbit, discussed the company's recent milestone in producing protein crystals in space. BioOrbit launched a record-breaking mission to the International Space Station, achieving the largest scale production of protein crystals in a microgravity environment by one to two orders of magnitude. 
King spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe Must Increase Space Autonomy: ESA's Aschbacher
summary:
The man who oversees Europe's space agenda says the continent needs to do much more as it pursues greater autonomy in orbit.
Europe is trailing the US in efforts to develop a space economy, underscored by SpaceX's mega IPO in June. Josef Aschbacher, Director General of the European Space Agency, spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Vanke Says First-Half Loss May Widen to $2.2 Billion
summary: China Vanke Co. said its first-half loss could reach $2.2 billion due to low gross margins on property development projects and asset impairment charges.
